title: Amplification of phage genes from lysates and environmental samples
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"dflpk","text":"From Sullivan M., Lindell D., Lee J., Thompson L., Bielawski J., Chisholm S. Prevalence and Evolution of Core Photosystem II Genes in Marine Cyanobacterial Viruses and Their Hosts.PLOS Biology, 2006 4(8):e234. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":180,"length":14}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":77,"length":103}],"data":[]},{"key":"eukkr","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b2ho9","text":"Please see the published manuscript for additional information.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"BF17E681D4F3406FB666D88C06945ABF","url":"http://journals.plos.org/plosbiology/article?id=10.1371/journal.pbio.0040234"}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"c5ufr","text":"PCR reactions were performed with Taq DNA polymerase and dNTPs from New England Biolabs (Beverly, MA) or Invitrogen (Carlsbad, CA) and carried out with a PTC-100 or PTC-200 DNA Engine (MJ Research, Waltham, MA) or a Robocycler Gradient 96 (Stratagene, La Jolla, CA). Template amounts were: 10 ng genomic DNA for Prochlorococcus and Synechococcus, 1 µL filtrate for cyanophages, and 2 µL filtrate for environmental samples. PCR primers and amplification reaction conditions are shown in Suppl. Tables 3 and 4.              ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":34,"length":3},{"style":"italic","offset":312,"length":15},{"style":"italic","offset":332,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9a1j9","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fa3sp","text":"The psbA gene from all sources was amplified using primer pair psbA-F/R [62] and PCR protocol A (see Suppl. Tables 3 and 4). Four reactions were conducted with each template, and the products were pooled and analyzed by agarose gel electrophoresis. Primer psbA-R falls on the intron region in S-PM2 (see ref. [29]). Therefore for efficient amplification of phage psbA genes that may contain introns, and for increased sensitivity, we used the Pro-psbA-F/R primer set and protocol B in nested PCR reactions when no PCR product was visible from cyanophage lysates and environmental filtrates. To reduce the incidence of heteroduplex formation, amplification products from environmental samples were subjected to reconditioning PCR [63]: initial PCR products were diluted 1:10, then amplified using protocol A but for only 3 cycles.            ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":4,"length":4},{"style":"italic","offset":63,"length":4},{"style":"italic","offset":256,"length":4},{"style":"italic","offset":363,"length":4},{"style":"italic","offset":447,"length":4}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8802r","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2m1uu","text":"The psbD gene from Prochlorococcus, Synechococcus, and cyanophages was amplified using primer pair psbD-54F/psbD-308R and protocol D.  However when product yield was low or absent semi-nested PCR was carried out as follows. Amplification was first conducted using primer pair psbD-26F/psbD-308R and protocol C. Four reactions were conducted with each template, the products were pooled, diluted 1:10, and used as templates for a second round of amplification using primer pair psbD-54F/psbD-308R and protocol D. psbD from environmental samples was amplified using primer pair psbD-26F/psbD-308R and protocol C and subjected to reconditioning PCR as for psbA (see above).          ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":4,"length":4},{"style":"italic","offset":19,"length":15},{"style":"italic","offset":36,"length":13},{"style":"italic","offset":99,"length":4},{"style":"italic","offset":108,"length":4},{"style":"italic","offset":276,"length":4},{"style":"italic","offset":285,"length":4},{"style":"italic","offset":477,"length":4},{"style":"italic","offset":486,"length":4},{"style":"italic","offset":512,"length":4},{"style":"italic","offset":576,"length":4},{"style":"italic","offset":585,"length":4},{"style":"italic","offset":653,"length":4}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7ma8v","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7ktth","text":"In preparation for sequencing, PCR products were either purified directly using the QIAquick PCR Purification Kit (Qiagen) or separated on an agarose gel and then purified using the QIAquick Gel Extraction Kit (Qiagen).            ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"224f3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"20m3v","text":"To confirm that the absence of psbA or psbD PCR products from phage was not simply due to a lack of amplifiable phage DNA, we screened phage lysates for known phage genes: g20 (for myoviruses) and DNApol (for podoviruses). g20 was amplified using primer pair g20-F/R and protocol E, and DNApol using primer pair DNApol-F/R and protocol F, both with 1µL filtrate from cyanophage isolates. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":31,"length":4},{"style":"italic","offset":39,"length":4},{"style":"italic","offset":172,"length":3},{"style":"italic","offset":197,"length":6},{"style":"italic","offset":223,"length":3},{"style":"italic","offset":287,"length":6}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bvtli","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fqj3u","text":"Supplementary Table 3.
